mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are positive in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer and housing demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An index value above 105 for the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing and utility output capacity, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO > 105 correlates with negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations during high-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% while the CPI index remains above 315, persistent inflation forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive forward returns in >50% of cases over a 4-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly expand aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in >55% of monthly observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annually signals a severe demand contraction that triggers recession fears, causing immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.5% is followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances during contraction phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while the unemployment rate falls indicates a healthy supply-demand balance without inflationary wage pressures, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO/UNRATE divergence signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 or positive returns in <45% of triggered months over a 3-year window.
